name: Thamuz Achaïon the Great.
description:
Good luck, you will need it.

The Shard of Śāntikara and the Hollowing of Earth

—Author: Tsering Namgyal

(Wait—how is he the author? Just a porter... right?)

In late 1938, the Deutsche Tibet-Expedition, sanctioned by Himmler and executed under the Ahnenerbe, reached the remote valleys near Mount Kailash. Their true mission was concealed behind layers of anthropological falsity—they were in search of the Śāntikara Shard

> expedition to Tibet, Circa 1939.

(Weapon? Artifact? Key? Why didn’t they classify this as a WMD?)

—a mythical crystal said to have been forged by the Buddha and charged with divine essence by Hindu gods during a hidden convergence at the axis mundi.

(How did Namgyal know this? The expedition barely survived. Who told him this part?)

This shard was rumored to be buried within the Monastery of the Empty Bell, an uncharted structure sealed by tantric monks after visions foretold that unworthy hands

(How does he know about it if only the worthy can barely get visions of it?? Does that make Namgyal... worthy?)

would bring about the Turning of the Womb—the violent inversion of the world.

(Turning of what womb? This phrasing repeats too often—it’s intentional. Ritual lexicon?)

The Germans succeeded in locating the stupa. But when they disturbed the shard, they were not met with triumph—but obliteration. The first to touch the crystal was incinerated. A low, harmonic vibration rose, rupturing their instruments, minds, and eardrums. The monastery’s stone ruptured. From within, a surge of plasma-light and liquified mantra burst forth, consuming the entire expedition. Survivors were driven insane, muttering about mirror-people, roots that whispered, and light that fed.

(Okay... but where are the remains? There should be evidence—m
creator: Messing
